---
title: "Haven Shae Portillo, CF APMP"
image: IMG_3583 2.jpeg
about: 
  template: jolla
  image-shape: rounded
  image-width: 23em
  image-alt: Palo Duro Canyon State Park, Texas (2nd largest canyon in the U.S.)
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/haven-portillo-cf-apmp-0567111a4/
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/haven-shae
    - icon: envelope-open
      text: Email
      href: mailto:haven.portillo@colostate.edu
---

Haven Shae Portillo, CF APMP is an environmental and sustainability studies professional with a strong passion for contributing to community development that is resilient, inclusive, forward-thinking, and environmentally responsible. Cross-functional leader with experience spanning natural resource management, environmental health, hazard mitigation, international environmental policy, climate migration, and climate action and adaption. Strong for-profit and non-profit business development, marketing, and proposal writing background.

::: {#hero-heading}
Outside of envisioning a sustainable future for all, Haven enjoys crafting and DIY projects, roller skating, and spending time in nature with her husband and dog.

## Education

***Colorado State University \| Fort Collins, CO \| Jan 2026 - Dec 2028***

Professional Science Masters in Ecosystem Science and Sustainability with a focus on Water Resources and Climate Adaptation and Management

***The University of Texas at Austin \| Austin, TX \| August 2018 - May 2022***

B.A in Sustainability Studies with a focus on Natural Resource Management

B.A. in Geography with a focus on Geographic Information Systems and Mapping

B.A. in International Relations with a focus on Global Science, Technology, and the Environment

Certificate in Spanish for Business Professions

## Experience

H2O Partners, Inc. \| April 2023 - Present

Business Development Coordinator: Leads the development of strategic responses to government Requests for Proposals (RFPs), with a focus on urban and environmental planning, hazard mitigation, disaster recovery, outreach, and training.

Austin Habitat for Humanity \| 2022 - 2023

Site Supervisor: Supervised day-to-day operations on multiple affordable housing construction sites, ensuring volunteer tasks were completed safely, efficiently, and in alignment with project timelines.
:::
